waves.name: Interface & Contract Definitions
waves.wave_description: Define ALL interfaces upfront to freeze contracts and enable Phase 1 Wave 2 parallel implementation

waves.name: Core Package Implementations
waves.wave_description: Implement all four core packages in parallel using frozen Wave 1 interfaces

waves.name: Command Implementation & Core Integration
waves.wave_description: Create the foundational idpbuilder push command that integrates all Phase 1 packages into a functional CLI tool with basic progress reporting

waves.name: Advanced Configuration Features
waves.wave_description: Add registry override capability and environment variable support with proper precedence handling

waves.name: Security & Validation
waves.wave_description: Implement comprehensive input validation, command injection prevention, SSRF protection, and production-grade error handling

metadata.project_description: Implement IDPBuilder OCI image-push command for pushing from the docker Daemon image storage to the idpbuilder Gitea registry located by default at https://gitea.cnoe.localtest.me:8443/ but should also have a --registry flag that allows you to specify a registry overiding the one that is used by the image name image. Core functionality includes registry authentication for the push command taking in a --username and --password flag... where the username defaults to giteaadmin and the password should support a large number of characters including special characters. We also need to support a -k or --insecure flag that bypasses the tls/ssl certificate checking like with curl -k. This work specifically excludes and image build functionality. Lets focus solely on push with a workflow that depends on the docker command and daemon and the image already being pushed and available. Lets also avoid doing any certificate handling. Do not do anything tricky witth cerficates, I dont need you to manage them, we just need to implement the --insecure flat to begin with. We can manage cert bundles/exporting certifcates used by gitea later. This is just the start of the work.
metadata.notes: State transition: SPAWN_CODE_REVIEWERS_EFFORT_PLANNING → WAITING_FOR_EFFORT_PLANS - Effort 2.2.2 implementation plan created (923 lines), waiting for Code Reviewer completion

effort-1.theme: CLI command implementation with Phase 1 integration
effort-1.scope: Command structure, flag definitions, pipeline orchestration, basic error handling

effort-2.theme: Enhanced progress tracking and console output formatting
effort-2.scope: Layer-by-layer progress, thread-safe updates, summary statistics, verbose/normal modes
